name: ExtensionResearchStudy_Focus
title: R5: focus additional types
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `ResearchStudy.focus` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`ResearchStudy.focus` 0..* `CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Medication,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/MedicinalProductDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/SubstanceDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/EvidenceVariable)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `ResearchStudy.focus` 0..* `CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Medication,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/MedicinalProductDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/SubstanceDefinition,http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/EvidenceVariable)`
*  R4B: `ResearchStudy.focus` 0..* `CodeableConcept`
*  R4: `ResearchStudy.focus` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `ResearchStudy.focus` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `ResearchStudy.focus` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `ResearchStudy.focus` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.

element.short: R5: Reference to a concept (by class) (new)
element.comment:
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is part of an existing definition because parent element `CodeableReference` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `CodeableReference` is not mapped.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
